title: GitHub - KanishkNoir/cognikernel: Persistent, structured project memory for Claude Code and Codex - event-sourced, typed, fail-open. Your agent stops re-deciding what you already decided.
excerpt: Persistent, structured project memory for Claude Code and Codex - event-sourced, typed, fail-open. Your agent stops re-deciding what you already decided. - KanishkNoir/cognikernel
content_html:
Persistent, structured project memory for Claude Code — and Codex. CogniKernel watches a coding session through its hook surfaces, extracts the decisions, constraints, and abandoned approaches worth keeping, consolidates them into an event-sourced store, and injects them back as a compact context block the next time you work — so the agent stops re-deciding what you already decided. The store is keyed on the project path, so memory made in one tool travels to the other.

It is not a vector-database wrapper. It is an event-sourced log of typed memory with lexical-primary retrieval, write-time consolidation, and a fail-open reliability spine designed never to break your session.

And there is no LLM in the loop. Most memory tools work by sending your transcripts to a generative model to "summarize what mattered" — another API key, per-session token cost, added latency, and your session content leaving the machine. CogniKernel treats extraction as classification, not generation: a deterministic sanitize → classify → consolidate pipeline, with two small fine-tuned encoder models (~130 MB ONNX, run locally on CPU in milliseconds) scoring salience and detecting when a new decision supersedes an old one. No API calls, no tokens billed, nothing leaves your machine. The only LLM involved is the coding agent you already run — CogniKernel makes it remember.

Naming: CogniKernel is the project; memlora (package memlora-edge) is the Python module and CLI it ships as — the working name the code grew up under. One project, two names: memlora init, memlora doctor, etc.

The memory loop

Everything CogniKernel does is one loop: observe → extract → consolidate → store → retrieve → assemble → inject. The left rail captures; the right rail recalls; the spine underneath keeps both honest.

   +========================= CLAUDE CODE SESSION =========================+
   | working memory  -  the context window the agent reasons in            |
   +--------------+-----------------------------------------+--------------+
                  | Stop hook captures transcript           | inject block
                  v                                         ^
   +--------------+---------------+         +---------------+--------------+
   | [1] EXTRACTION PIPELINE      |         | [6] COMPRESSION + INJECTION  |
   |   sanitize -> classify ->    |         |   authority-weighted budget, |
   |   salience (ONNX) ->         |         |   drop-to-fit (keep every    |
   |   decision-key + contracts   |         |   constraint) -> block       |
   +--------------+---------------+         +---------------+--------------+
                  | enqueue                                 | rank + fit
                  v                                         ^
   +--------------+---------------+         +---------------+--------------+
   | [2] WORKER + CONSOLIDATION   |         | [5] RETRIEVAL                |
   |   claim -> delta-merge ->    |         |   FTS5 BM25 + dense -> RRF   |
   |   supersede (latest-wins) -> |         |   prohibition_search (K1)    |
   |   project   (idempotent)     |         |   skeleton graph (PageRank)  |
   +--------------+---------------+         +---------------+--------------+
                  | persist (atomic)                        | recall
                  v                                         ^
   +--------------+-----------------------------------------+--------------+
   | [3] EVENT-SOURCED STORE   *   SQLite (WAL)                            |
   | typed events | evidence | provenance | FTS5 | embeddings | ledger     |
   +----------------------------------------------------------------------+
   | [4] RELIABILITY SPINE   atomic migrations | idempotent replay |       |
   | doctor --strict | fail-open hooks | import-linter | CI gate           |
   +----------------------------------------------------------------------+

The four hook surfaces

CogniKernel attaches to a session at four points. Each is fail-open — if memory is unavailable or errors, the hook logs at WARNING, returns cleanly, and the session continues.

Surface Hook Authority What it does
Session block SessionStart advisory injects the canonical decisions/constraints/skeleton block
CK-1 recall UserPromptSubmit advisory surfaces prompt-relevant memory, dual-evidence gated, dedup'd via render ledger
Read/Edit gate PreToolUse hard / JIT read-efficiency gate on Read/Grep; just-in-time prohibition surfacing on Write/Edit (K2)
Capture Stop side-effect extracts and persists decisions — you never write memory to CLAUDE.md by hand

What gets remembered

Memory is typed, not free-text chunks. The type drives ranking, rendering, and supersession:

  • DECISION — a choice that was made ("use Redis for the rate limiter")
  • CONSTRAINT_HARD / CONSTRAINT_SOFT — rules, graded by deontic force
  • APPROACH_ABANDONED_DO_NOT_RETRY — a dead end, kept in the graveyard so the agent doesn't re-attempt it
  • conventions, config facts, schema decisions (canonical role keys)

A decision key lets a later restatement supersede an earlier one (latest-wins), so the store self-consolidates instead of accumulating contradictions. An optional cross-encoder adds semantic supersession when the encoder backend is installed.

Retrieval

Lexical-primary, with dense as a fused signal — never pure vector:

  • Hybrid core — FTS5 BM25 ∪ optional dense embeddings → Reciprocal Rank Fusion
  • prohibition_search — a type-restricted lexical pool so a "do not do X" rule can't be crowded out by topically-similar prose at the moment you're about to do X
  • Skeleton — an AST symbol graph ranked by PageRank; find_related unions semantic (embedding) neighbours with import-graph-adjacent events to surface what a change touches (the semantic axis needs the embedding extra)
  • Golden-record consolidation at read — latest-wins reconciliation so recall returns one coherent answer, not a pile of revisions

What it saves you

Benchmarked in a three-arm comparison — CogniKernel vs flat curated notes vs no memory — with real agent sessions across four multi-session projects:

  • File reads: the universal win. The CogniKernel arm made the fewest file reads in every project — typically 2–4× fewer (23 vs 63, 16 vs 47/53, 40 vs 89/83), and in the best case 3 reads vs 29 because the injected block + AST skeleton carried the whole repo's shape. Fewer reads means fewer tool round-trips and more of the context window left for actual work — your session gets longer before compaction, not just cheaper.
  • Tokens: up to ~20% cheaper where memory matters. Price-weighted token cost (cache-write 1.25×, cache-read 0.1×, output 5×) came out 18–23% lower on projects with evolving decisions and cross-file dependencies — and roughly a wash on small implementation-heavy projects where the code itself is cheap to re-read. We publish the honest number, not the raw-token one (raw sums look ~30–40% better, but ~95% of any session's bill is discounted cache-read).
  • Recall instead of re-derivation. Where memory earns its keep is projects whose state is too large, too evolving, or too long-lived to re-derive cheaply: the agent starts already knowing the decisions, constraints, and dead ends, instead of spending the first quarter of the session rediscovering them.

Reliability — the spine

The system is designed to degrade legibly, never silently:

  • Atomic migrations — each numbered migration applies its body + version bump in one transaction; safe to crash mid-script
  • Idempotent replay — a re-run worker job can't double-count or drift decay (evidence-provenance guard)
  • Fail-open hooks — every surface swallows its own failure and logs at WARNING; silence never reads as success
  • memlora doctor --strict — per-subsystem health (schema, FTS5, embeddings, symbols, worker queue); non-zero exit when degraded
  • Architecture enforcement — import-linter layered contracts, guarded by a meta-test so a typo can't silently disable them
  • CI promotion gate — lint + full suite (incl. tests/reliability/ failure-injection) on every PR; see CONTRIBUTING.md

Cross-platform (Codex)

The store is platform-neutral — one SQLite DB per logical project, so Claude Code and Codex working in the same directory share one memory. Project resolution is alias-aware: C:\repo and /mnt/c/repo resolve to the same store, so memory follows the checkout across Windows, WSL, and native mounts; for genuinely different checkout paths, an opt-in project_identity key in .memlora/config.toml pins them to one shared store. Codex reads memory through the registered MCP server (get_session_state / recall); the capture direction is pull-based, because Codex has no Stop-hook equivalent:

  • memlora codex-sync <project> scans ~/.codex/sessions for rollouts whose recorded cwd maps to the project and captures the delta through the same extraction pipeline (a rollout→transcript adapter is the only Codex-specific code; delta/dedup/idempotency are shared and unchanged).
  • Automatic at the handoff — Claude's SessionStart drains pending Codex rollouts before building the block, and the MCP server's queue drainer pulls new rollouts each cycle, so a live Claude session picks up Codex-side decisions without waiting for the next session; on the Codex side, init writes an AGENTS.md instruction + a ck-sync skill so Codex pulls at session start.
  • init provisions both — .mcp.json (Claude) and .codex/config.toml (with the server's cwd + project env pinned) + AGENTS.md (Codex), idempotently and without clobbering existing settings.
  • memlora doctor reports a codex health line (sessions dir + rollout count, or "nothing to sync" — Codex is optional, so its absence is healthy).

A decision made in Codex reaches the next Claude session's block, and vice versa. The action-point surfaces (CK-1, PreToolUse gate) are Claude-only — Codex has no per-prompt/per-tool hook — so on Codex the loop degrades to the shared block + MCP recall.

Interfaces

MCP tools (the session block is injected automatically; these are for targeted use): recall · find_related · skeleton · get_session_state

CLI:

  • memlora init <project> — register the project and install the session hooks
  • memlora doctor [--strict] <project> — subsystem health report
  • memlora codex-sync <project> — capture Codex CLI sessions for this project
  • memlora install-heads — install the trained encoder artifacts (salience + cross-encoder ONNX bodies): downloaded from the heads-v1 release and sha256-verified, or copied from a local models/ export when present
  • memlora show <project> / memlora reset <project> — inspect / clear stored memory

Quickstart

uv sync                      # core (lexical-only)
uv sync --extra embedding    # + dense retrieval (fastembed + numpy)

uv run memlora init .            # register this project + install hooks
uv run memlora doctor .          # subsystem health

uv run memlora install-heads     # optional: trained encoder heads (~270 MB download);
                                 # without them extraction/supersession fall back to
                                 # the legacy/lexical path — everything still works

Then start a Claude Code session in the project — the memory block appears automatically at session start, and decisions are captured when the session ends.

Project layout

src/memlora/
  integration/   hooks, CLI, MCP server, session/worker orchestration
  extraction/    sanitize -> classify -> salience -> decision-key pipeline
  delta/         delta-merge + supersession (latest-wins; cross-encoder optional)
  retrieval/     hybrid BM25 + dense -> RRF
  storage/       event-sourced SQLite, FTS5, migrations, render ledger
  embedding/     optional dense vectors (fastembed)
  symbols/       AST skeleton + PageRank graph
  compression/   authority-weighted drop-to-fit budget
  injection/     block template assembly
  model.py       Event — the dependency-free domain primitive
tests/
  unit/          per-subsystem
  reliability/   crash-replay · worker-contention · corrupt-input injection

Status

Schema v18 (includes the Codex cross-platform capture). Architecture contracts: 3 kept / 0 broken. CI gate: lint + full suite on Ubuntu (3.11/3.12) and Windows. See CONTRIBUTING.md for the Definition of Done that gates every change.

CogniKernel watches a coding session through its hook surfaces, extracts the decisions, constraints, and abandoned approaches worth keeping, consolidates them into an event-sourced store, and injects them back as a compact context block the next time you work — so the agent stops re-deciding what you already decided. The store is keyed on the project path, so memory made in one tool travels to the other.

sentences.en: Persistent, structured project memory for Claude Code — and Codex.
sentences.zh: 用于 Claude Code 和 Codex 的持久、结构化项目内存。

sentences.en: CogniKernel watches a coding session through its hook surfaces, extracts the decisions, constraints, and abandoned approaches worth keeping, consolidates them into an event-sourced store, and injects them back as a compact context block the next time you work — so the agent stops re-deciding what you already decided.
sentences.zh: CogniKernel 通过其钩子表面监视编码会话，提取值得保留的决策、约束和废弃方法，将它们合并到事件源存储中，并在您下次工作时将它们作为紧凑的上下文块注入回来 - 这样代理就不再重新决定您已经决定的内容。

sentences.en: The store is keyed on the project path, so memory made in one tool travels to the other.
sentences.zh: 该存储以项目路径为关键，因此一个工具中生成的内存会传输到另一个工具中。

blocks.html_en: It is not a vector-database wrapper. It is an event-sourced log of typed memory with lexical-primary retrieval, write-time consolidation, and a fail-open reliability spine designed never to break your session.

sentences.en: It is not a vector-database wrapper.
sentences.zh: 它不是矢量数据库包装器。

sentences.en: It is an event-sourced log of typed memory with lexical-primary retrieval, write-time consolidation, and a fail-open reliability spine designed never to break your session.
sentences.zh: 它是类型化内存的事件源日志，具有词汇主检索、写入时合并和故障开放可靠性脊柱，旨在永远不会中断您的会话。

Most memory tools work by sending your transcripts to a generative model to "summarize what mattered" — another API key, per-session token cost, added latency, and your session content leaving the machine. CogniKernel treats extraction as classification, not generation: a deterministic sanitize → classify → consolidate pipeline, with two small fine-tuned encoder models (~130 MB ONNX, run locally on CPU in milliseconds) scoring salience and detecting when a new decision supersedes an old one. No API calls, no tokens billed, nothing leaves your machine. The only LLM involved is the coding agent you already run — CogniKernel makes it remember.

sentences.en: And there is no LLM in the loop.
sentences.zh: 并且循环中没有LLM。

sentences.en: Most memory tools work by sending your transcripts to a generative model to "summarize what mattered" — another API key, per-session token cost, added latency, and your session content leaving the machine.
sentences.zh: 大多数记忆工具的工作原理是将你的记录发送到生成模型以“总结重要的事情”——另一个 API 密钥、每个会话令牌成本、增加的延迟以及离开机器的会话内容。

sentences.en: CogniKernel treats extraction as classification, not generation: a deterministic sanitize → classify → consolidate pipeline, with two small fine-tuned encoder models (~130 MB ONNX, run locally on CPU in milliseconds) scoring salience and detecting when a new decision supersedes an old one.
sentences.zh: CogniKernel 将提取视为分类，而不是生成：确定性的清理 → 分类 → 整合管道，使用两个小型微调编码器模型（约 130 MB ONNX，以毫秒为单位在 CPU 上本地运行）对显着性进行评分并检测新决策何时取代旧决策。

sentences.en: No API calls, no tokens billed, nothing leaves your machine.
sentences.zh: 没有 API 调用，没有代币计费，任何东西都不会离开您的机器。

sentences.en: The only LLM involved is the coding agent you already run — CogniKernel makes it remember.
sentences.zh: 唯一涉及的 LLM 是您已经运行的编码代理 — CogniKernel 让它记住。

blocks.html_en: The memory loop

sentences.en: The memory loop
sentences.zh: 记忆循环

blocks.html_en: Everything CogniKernel does is one loop: observe → extract → consolidate → store → retrieve → assemble → inject. The left rail captures; the right rail recalls; the spine underneath keeps both honest.

sentences.en: Everything CogniKernel does is one loop: observe → extract → consolidate → store → retrieve → assemble → inject.
sentences.zh: CogniKernel 所做的一切都是一个循环：观察→提取→合并→存储→检索→组装→注入。

sentences.en: The left rail captures; the right rail recalls; the spine underneath keeps both honest.
sentences.zh: 左导轨捕获；右导轨召回；下面的脊柱使两者都保持诚实。

blocks.html_en: The four hook surfaces

sentences.en: The four hook surfaces
sentences.zh: 四个钩面

blocks.html_en: CogniKernel attaches to a session at four points. Each is fail-open — if memory is unavailable or errors, the hook logs at WARNING, returns cleanly, and the session continues.

sentences.en: CogniKernel attaches to a session at four points.
sentences.zh: CogniKernel 在四个点附加到会话。

sentences.en: Each is fail-open — if memory is unavailable or errors, the hook logs at WARNING, returns cleanly, and the session continues.
sentences.zh: 每个都是失败打开的 - 如果内存不可用或出现错误，则挂钩会记录为“警告”，干净地返回，并且会话继续。

sentences.zh: 硬/JIT

sentences.zh: 捕获

blocks.html_en: Stop
side-effect

sentences.en: side-effect
sentences.zh: 副作用

blocks.html_en: extracts and persists decisions — you never write memory to CLAUDE.md by hand

sentences.en: extracts and persists decisions — you never write memory to CLAUDE.md by hand
sentences.zh: 提取并保存决策 - 你永远不会手动将内存写入 CLAUDE.md

blocks.html_en: What gets remembered

sentences.en: What gets remembered
sentences.zh: 什么被记住

blocks.html_en: Memory is typed, not free-text chunks. The type drives ranking, rendering, and supersession:

sentences.en: Memory is typed, not free-text chunks.
sentences.zh: 记忆是键入的，而不是自由文本块。

sentences.en: The type drives ranking, rendering, and supersession:
sentences.zh: 类型驱动排名、渲染和取代：

blocks.html_en: • DECISION — a choice that was made ("use Redis for the rate limiter")

sentences.en: DECISION — a choice that was made ("use Redis for the rate limiter")
sentences.zh: DECISION — 做出的选择（“使用 Redis 作为速率限制器”）

blocks.html_en: • CONSTRAINT_HARD / CONSTRAINT_SOFT — rules, graded by deontic force

sentences.en: CONSTRAINT_HARD / CONSTRAINT_SOFT — rules, graded by deontic force
sentences.zh: CONSTRAINT_HARD / CONSTRAINT_SOFT — 规则，按道义力分级

blocks.html_en: • APPROACH_ABANDONED_DO_NOT_RETRY — a dead end, kept in the graveyard so the agent doesn't re-attempt it

sentences.en: APPROACH_ABANDONED_DO_NOT_RETRY — a dead end, kept in the graveyard so the agent doesn't re-attempt it
sentences.zh: APPROACH_ABANDONED_DO_NOT_RETRY — 死胡同，保留在墓地中，以便代理不会重新尝试

blocks.html_en: • conventions, config facts, schema decisions (canonical role keys)

sentences.en: conventions, config facts, schema decisions (canonical role keys)
sentences.zh: 约定、配置事实、模式决策（规范角色键）

blocks.html_en: A decision key lets a later restatement supersede an earlier one (latest-wins), so the store self-consolidates instead of accumulating contradictions. An optional cross-encoder adds semantic supersession when the encoder backend is installed.

sentences.en: A decision key lets a later restatement supersede an earlier one (latest-wins), so the store self-consolidates instead of accumulating contradictions.
sentences.zh: 决策键让稍后的重述取代先前的重述（最新获胜），因此商店会自我巩固而不是积累矛盾。

sentences.en: An optional cross-encoder adds semantic supersession when the encoder backend is installed.
sentences.zh: 安装编码器后端时，可选的交叉编码器会添加语义取代。

blocks.html_en: Retrieval

sentences.en: Retrieval
sentences.zh: 检索

blocks.html_en: Lexical-primary, with dense as a fused signal — never pure vector:

sentences.en: Lexical-primary, with dense as a fused signal — never pure vector:
sentences.zh: 词汇主要，具有稠密的融合信号 — 绝不是纯向量：

blocks.html_en: • Hybrid core — FTS5 BM25 ∪ optional dense embeddings → Reciprocal Rank Fusion

sentences.en: Hybrid core — FTS5 BM25 ∪ optional dense embeddings → Reciprocal Rank Fusion
sentences.zh: 混合核心 — FTS5 BM25 ∪ 可选密集嵌入 → 倒数秩融合

blocks.html_en: • prohibition_search — a type-restricted lexical pool so a "do not do X" rule can't be crowded out by topically-similar prose at the moment you're about to do X

sentences.en: prohibition_search — a type-restricted lexical pool so a "do not do X" rule can't be crowded out by topically-similar prose at the moment you're about to do X
sentences.zh: hibition_search — 类型限制的词汇池，因此在您即将执行 X 操作时，“不要执行 X”规则不会被主题相似的散文所挤出

blocks.html_en: • Skeleton — an AST symbol graph ranked by PageRank; find_related unions semantic (embedding) neighbours with import-graph-adjacent events to surface what a change touches (the semantic axis needs the embedding extra)

sentences.en: Skeleton — an AST symbol graph ranked by PageRank; find_related unions semantic (embedding) neighbours with import-graph-adjacent events to surface what a change touches (the semantic axis needs the embedding extra)
sentences.zh: Skeleton — 按 PageRank 排名的 AST 符号图； find_ related unions 语义（嵌入）邻居与导入图相邻事件来显示更改所涉及的内容（语义轴需要额外的嵌入）

blocks.html_en: • Golden-record consolidation at read — latest-wins reconciliation so recall returns one coherent answer, not a pile of revisions

sentences.en: Golden-record consolidation at read — latest-wins reconciliation so recall returns one coherent answer, not a pile of revisions
sentences.zh: 阅读时的黄金记录整合——最新胜利的协调，因此回忆会返回一个连贯的答案，而不是一堆修订

blocks.html_en: What it saves you

sentences.en: What it saves you
sentences.zh: 它能为您节省什么

blocks.html_en: Benchmarked in a three-arm comparison — CogniKernel vs flat curated notes vs no memory — with real agent sessions across four multi-session projects:

sentences.en: Benchmarked in a three-arm comparison — CogniKernel vs flat curated notes vs no memory — with real agent sessions across four multi-session projects:
sentences.zh: 在三臂比较中进行基准测试 - CogniKernel 与平面策划笔记 vs 无记忆 - 在四个多会话项目中进行真实代理会话：

blocks.html_en: • File reads: the universal win. The CogniKernel arm made the fewest file reads in every project — typically 2–4× fewer (23 vs 63, 16 vs 47/53, 40 vs 89/83), and in the best case 3 reads vs 29 because the injected block + AST skeleton carried the whole repo's shape. Fewer reads means fewer tool round-trips and more of the context window left for actual work — your session gets longer before compaction, not just cheaper.

sentences.en: File reads: the universal win.
sentences.zh: 文件内容如下：普遍胜利。

sentences.en: The CogniKernel arm made the fewest file reads in every project — typically 2–4× fewer (23 vs 63, 16 vs 47/53, 40 vs 89/83), and in the best case 3 reads vs 29 because the injected block + AST skeleton carried the whole repo's shape.
sentences.zh: CogniKernel 手臂在每个项目中进行的文件读取次数最少，通常少 2-4 倍（23 vs 63、16 vs 47/53、40 vs 89/83），在最好的情况下，读取次数为 3 vs 29，因为注入的块 + AST 骨架承载了整个存储库的形状。

sentences.en: Fewer reads means fewer tool round-trips and more of the context window left for actual work — your session gets longer before compaction, not just cheaper.
sentences.zh: 更少的读取意味着更少的工具往返和更多的上下文窗口留给实际工作——你的会话在压缩之前会变得更长，而不仅仅是更便宜。

blocks.html_en: • Tokens: up to ~20% cheaper where memory matters. Price-weighted token cost (cache-write 1.25×, cache-read 0.1×, output 5×) came out 18–23% lower on projects with evolving decisions and cross-file dependencies — and roughly a wash on small implementation-heavy projects where the code itself is cheap to re-read. We publish the honest number, not the raw-token one (raw sums look ~30–40% better, but ~95% of any session's bill is discounted cache-read).

sentences.en: Tokens: up to ~20% cheaper where memory matters.
sentences.zh: 令牌：在内存很重要的情况下最多便宜 20%。

sentences.en: Price-weighted token cost (cache-write 1.25×, cache-read 0.1×, output 5×) came out 18–23% lower on projects with evolving decisions and cross-file dependencies — and roughly a wash on small implementation-heavy projects where the code itself is cheap to re-read.
sentences.zh: 对于具有不断变化的决策和跨文件依赖关系的项目，价格加权代币成本（缓存写入 1.25×、缓存读取 0.1×、输出 5×）降低了 18-23%，而对于代码本身重读成本低廉的小型实施密集型项目来说，价格加权代币成本降低了 18-23%。

sentences.en: We publish the honest number, not the raw-token one (raw sums look ~30–40% better, but ~95% of any session's bill is discounted cache-read).
sentences.zh: 我们发布诚实的数字，而不是原始令牌的数字（原始数字看起来好约 30-40%，但任何会话的账单的约 95% 都是折扣缓存读取的）。

blocks.html_en: • Recall instead of re-derivation. Where memory earns its keep is projects whose state is too large, too evolving, or too long-lived to re-derive cheaply: the agent starts already knowing the decisions, constraints, and dead ends, instead of spending the first quarter of the session rediscovering them.

sentences.en: Recall instead of re-derivation.
sentences.zh: 回忆而不是重新推导。

sentences.en: Where memory earns its keep is projects whose state is too large, too evolving, or too long-lived to re-derive cheaply: the agent starts already knowing the decisions, constraints, and dead ends, instead of spending the first quarter of the session rediscovering them.
sentences.zh: 记忆发挥作用的地方是那些状态太大、进化太多或寿命太长而无法廉价重新推导的项目：智能体开始已经知道决策、约束和死胡同，而不是花费会话的第一季度重新发现它们。

blocks.html_en: Reliability — the spine

sentences.en: Reliability — the spine
sentences.zh: 可靠性——脊柱

blocks.html_en: The system is designed to degrade legibly, never silently:

sentences.en: The system is designed to degrade legibly, never silently:
sentences.zh: 该系统被设计为清晰地降级，而不是静默降级：

blocks.html_en: • Atomic migrations — each numbered migration applies its body + version bump in one transaction; safe to crash mid-script

sentences.en: Atomic migrations — each numbered migration applies its body + version bump in one transaction; safe to crash mid-script
sentences.zh: 原子迁移——每个编号的迁移在一个事务中应用其主体+版本碰撞；脚本中途安全崩溃

blocks.html_en: • Idempotent replay — a re-run worker job can't double-count or drift decay (evidence-provenance guard)

sentences.en: Idempotent replay — a re-run worker job can't double-count or drift decay (evidence-provenance guard)
sentences.zh: 幂等重放——重新运行的工作人员工作不能重复计算或漂移衰减（证据来源守卫）

blocks.html_en: • Fail-open hooks — every surface swallows its own failure and logs at WARNING; silence never reads as success

sentences.en: Fail-open hooks — every surface swallows its own failure and logs at WARNING; silence never reads as success
sentences.zh: 故障打开钩子——每个表面都会吞下自己的故障并记录在警告处；沉默永远不会被解读为成功

blocks.html_en: • memlora doctor --strict — per-subsystem health (schema, FTS5, embeddings, symbols, worker queue); non-zero exit when degraded

sentences.en: memlora doctor --strict — per-subsystem health (schema, FTS5, embeddings, symbols, worker queue); non-zero exit when degraded
sentences.zh: memlora doctor --strict — 每个子系统的健康状况（模式、FTS5、嵌入、符号、工作队列）；降级时非零退出

blocks.html_en: • Architecture enforcement — import-linter layered contracts, guarded by a meta-test so a typo can't silently disable them

sentences.en: Architecture enforcement — import-linter layered contracts, guarded by a meta-test so a typo can't silently disable them
sentences.zh: 架构实施 - import-linter 分层合约，由元测试保护，因此拼写错误无法默默地禁用它们

blocks.html_en: • CI promotion gate — lint + full suite (incl. tests/reliability/ failure-injection) on every PR; see CONTRIBUTING.md

sentences.en: CI promotion gate — lint + full suite (incl.
sentences.zh: CI 推广门 — lint + 全套（包括

sentences.en: tests/reliability/ failure-injection) on every PR; see CONTRIBUTING.md
sentences.zh: 每个 PR 的测试/可靠性/故障注入）；参见 CONTRIBUTING.md

blocks.html_en: Cross-platform (Codex)

sentences.en: Cross-platform (Codex)
sentences.zh: 跨平台（法典）

blocks.html_en: The store is platform-neutral — one SQLite DB per logical project, so Claude Code and Codex working in the same directory share one memory. Project resolution is alias-aware: C:\repo and /mnt/c/repo resolve to the same store, so memory follows the checkout across Windows, WSL, and native mounts; for genuinely different checkout paths, an opt-in project_identity key in .memlora/config.toml pins them to one shared store. Codex reads memory through the registered MCP server (get_session_state / recall); the capture direction is pull-based, because Codex has no Stop-hook equivalent:

sentences.en: The store is platform-neutral — one SQLite DB per logical project, so Claude Code and Codex working in the same directory share one memory.
sentences.zh: 该存储是平台中立的 - 每个逻辑项目一个 SQLite DB，因此在同一目录中工作的 Claude Code 和 Codex 共享一个内存。

sentences.en: Project resolution is alias-aware: C:\repo and /mnt/c/repo resolve to the same store, so memory follows the checkout across Windows, WSL, and native mounts; for genuinely different checkout paths, an opt-in project_identity key in .memlora/config.toml pins them to one shared store.
sentences.zh: 项目解析是别名感知的：C:\repo 和 /mnt/c/repo 解析为同一存储，因此内存遵循跨 Windows、WSL 和本机安装的签出；对于真正不同的结帐路径，.memlora/config.toml 中的一个选择加入的 project_identity 键将它们固定到一个共享存储。

sentences.en: Codex reads memory through the registered MCP server (get_session_state / recall); the capture direction is pull-based, because Codex has no Stop-hook equivalent:
sentences.zh: Codex通过注册的MCP服务器读取内存（get_session_state/recall）；捕获方向是基于拉动的，因为 Codex 没有等效的 Stop-hook：

blocks.html_en: • memlora codex-sync <project> scans ~/.codex/sessions for rollouts whose recorded cwd maps to the project and captures the delta through the same extraction pipeline (a rollout→transcript adapter is the only Codex-specific code; delta/dedup/idempotency are shared and unchanged).

sentences.en: memlora codex-sync <project> scans ~/.codex/sessions for rollouts whose recorded cwd maps to the project and captures the delta through the same extraction pipeline (a rollout→transcript adapter is the only Codex-specific code; delta/dedup/idempotency are shared and unchanged).
sentences.zh: memlora codex-sync <project> 扫描 ~/.codex/sessions 中记录的 cwd 映射到项目的 rollout，并通过相同的提取管道捕获增量（rollout→transcript 适配器是唯一特定于 Codex 的代码；delta/dedup/idempotency 是共享的且未更改）。

blocks.html_en: • Automatic at the handoff — Claude's SessionStart drains pending Codex rollouts before building the block, and the MCP server's queue drainer pulls new rollouts each cycle, so a live Claude session picks up Codex-side decisions without waiting for the next session; on the Codex side, init writes an AGENTS.md instruction + a ck-sync skill so Codex pulls at session start.

sentences.en: Automatic at the handoff — Claude's SessionStart drains pending Codex rollouts before building the block, and the MCP server's queue drainer pulls new rollouts each cycle, so a live Claude session picks up Codex-side decisions without waiting for the next session; on the Codex side, init writes an AGENTS.md instruction + a ck-sync skill so Codex pulls at session start.
sentences.zh: 交接时自动 — Claude 的 SessionStart 在构建区块之前耗尽待处理的 Codex 推出，并且 MCP 服务器的队列耗尽器每个周期都会拉取新的推出，因此实时 Claude 会话无需等待下一个会话即可获取 Codex 端决策；在 Codex 方面，init 编写一条 AGENTS.md 指令 + 一个 ck-sync 技能，以便 Codex 在会话开始时拉取。

blocks.html_en: • init provisions both — .mcp.json (Claude) and .codex/config.toml (with the server's cwd + project env pinned) + AGENTS.md (Codex), idempotently and without clobbering existing settings.

sentences.en: init provisions both — .mcp.json (Claude) and .codex/config.toml (with the server's cwd + project env pinned) + AGENTS.md (Codex), idempotently and without clobbering existing settings.
sentences.zh: init 提供 — .mcp.json (Claude) 和 .codex/config.toml（固定服务器的 cwd + 项目环境）+ AGENTS.md (Codex)，幂等且不会破坏现有设置。

blocks.html_en: • memlora doctor reports a codex health line (sessions dir + rollout count, or "nothing to sync" — Codex is optional, so its absence is healthy).

sentences.en: memlora doctor reports a codex health line (sessions dir + rollout count, or "nothing to sync" — Codex is optional, so its absence is healthy).
sentences.zh: memlora doctor 报告了 Codex 运行状况线（会话目录 + 部署计数，或“没有要同步的内容”——Codex 是可选的，因此没有它是健康的）。

blocks.html_en: A decision made in Codex reaches the next Claude session's block, and vice versa. The action-point surfaces (CK-1, PreToolUse gate) are Claude-only — Codex has no per-prompt/per-tool hook — so on Codex the loop degrades to the shared block + MCP recall.

sentences.en: A decision made in Codex reaches the next Claude session's block, and vice versa.
sentences.zh: Codex 中做出的决定会到达下一个 Claude 会话的区块，反之亦然。

sentences.en: The action-point surfaces (CK-1, PreToolUse gate) are Claude-only — Codex has no per-prompt/per-tool hook — so on Codex the loop degrades to the shared block + MCP recall.
sentences.zh: 动作点表面（CK-1、PreToolUse 门）是 Claude-only 的 - Codex 没有每个提示/每个工具挂钩 - 因此在 Codex 上，循环降级为共享块 + MCP 调用。

blocks.html_en: Interfaces

sentences.en: Interfaces
sentences.zh: 接口

blocks.html_en: MCP tools (the session block is injected automatically; these are for targeted use): recall · find_related · skeleton · get_session_state

sentences.en: MCP tools (the session block is injected automatically; these are for targeted use): recall · find_related · skeleton · get_session_state
sentences.zh: MCP 工具（会话块自动注入；这些用于有针对性的使用）：recall · find_lated · sculpture · get_session_state

blocks.html_en: CLI:
• memlora init <project> — register the project and install the session hooks

sentences.en: memlora init <project> — register the project and install the session hooks
sentences.zh: memlora init <project> — 注册项目并安装会话挂钩

blocks.html_en: • memlora doctor [--strict] <project> — subsystem health report

sentences.en: memlora doctor [--strict] <project> — subsystem health report
sentences.zh: memlora doctor [--strict] <project> — 子系统健康报告

blocks.html_en: • memlora codex-sync <project> — capture Codex CLI sessions for this project

sentences.en: memlora codex-sync <project> — capture Codex CLI sessions for this project
sentences.zh: memlora codex-sync <project> — 捕获该项目的 Codex CLI 会话

blocks.html_en: • memlora install-heads — install the trained encoder artifacts (salience + cross-encoder ONNX bodies): downloaded from the heads-v1 release and sha256-verified, or copied from a local models/ export when present

sentences.en: memlora install-heads — install the trained encoder artifacts (salience + cross-encoder ONNX bodies): downloaded from the heads-v1 release and sha256-verified, or copied from a local models/ export when present
sentences.zh: memlora install-heads — 安装经过训练的编码器工件（salience + 交叉编码器 ONNX 主体）：从 Heads-v1 版本下载并经过 sha256 验证，或从本地模型/导出（如果存在）复制

blocks.html_en: • memlora show <project> / memlora reset <project> — inspect / clear stored memory

sentences.en: memlora show <project> / memlora reset <project> — inspect / clear stored memory
sentences.zh: memlora show <project> / memlora reset <project> — 检查/清除存储的内存

blocks.html_en: Quickstart

sentences.en: Quickstart
sentences.zh: 快速入门

blocks.html_en: Then start a Claude Code session in the project — the memory block appears automatically at session start, and decisions are captured when the session ends.

sentences.en: Then start a Claude Code session in the project — the memory block appears automatically at session start, and decisions are captured when the session ends.
sentences.zh: 然后在项目中启动 Claude Code 会话 - 内存块在会话开始时自动出现，并在会话结束时捕获决策。

blocks.html_en: Project layout

sentences.en: Project layout
sentences.zh: 项目布局

blocks.html_en: Status

sentences.en: Status
sentences.zh: 地位

blocks.html_en: Schema v18 (includes the Codex cross-platform capture). Architecture contracts: 3 kept / 0 broken. CI gate: lint + full suite on Ubuntu (3.11/3.12) and Windows. See CONTRIBUTING.md for the Definition of Done that gates every change.

sentences.en: Schema v18 (includes the Codex cross-platform capture).
sentences.zh: Schema v18（包括 Codex 跨平台捕获）。

sentences.en: Architecture contracts: 3 kept / 0 broken.
sentences.zh: 架构合同：3 个保留/0 个破坏。

sentences.en: CI gate: lint + full suite on Ubuntu (3.11/3.12) and Windows.
sentences.zh: CI 门：Ubuntu (3.11/3.12) 和 Windows 上的 lint + 完整套件。

sentences.en: See CONTRIBUTING.md for the Definition of Done that gates every change.
sentences.zh: 请参阅 CONTRIBUTING.md 了解控制每个更改的“完成”定义。

content_html_zh:
用于 Claude Code 和 Codex 的持久、结构化项目内存。 CogniKernel 通过其钩子表面监视编码会话，提取值得保留的决策、约束和废弃方法，将它们合并到事件源存储中，并在您下次工作时将它们作为紧凑的上下文块注入回来 - 这样代理就不再重新决定您已经决定的内容。 该存储以项目路径为关键，因此一个工具中生成的内存会传输到另一个工具中。

它不是矢量数据库包装器。 它是类型化内存的事件源日志，具有词汇主检索、写入时合并和故障开放可靠性脊柱，旨在永远不会中断您的会话。

并且循环中没有LLM。 大多数记忆工具的工作原理是将你的记录发送到生成模型以“总结重要的事情”——另一个 API 密钥、每个会话令牌成本、增加的延迟以及离开机器的会话内容。 CogniKernel 将提取视为分类，而不是生成：确定性的清理 → 分类 → 整合管道，使用两个小型微调编码器模型（约 130 MB ONNX，以毫秒为单位在 CPU 上本地运行）对显着性进行评分并检测新决策何时取代旧决策。 没有 API 调用，没有代币计费，任何东西都不会离开您的机器。 唯一涉及的 LLM 是您已经运行的编码代理 — CogniKernel 让它记住。

命名：CogniKernel是项目； memlora（memlora-edge 包）是 Python 模块，它提供的 CLI 是代码生成时的工作名称。 一个项目，两个名称：memlora init、memlora doctor 等。

记忆循环

CogniKernel 所做的一切都是一个循环：观察→提取→合并→存储→检索→组装→注入。 左导轨捕获；右导轨召回；下面的脊柱使两者都保持诚实。

   +========================= CLAUDE CODE SESSION =========================+
   | working memory  -  the context window the agent reasons in            |
   +--------------+-----------------------------------------+--------------+
                  | Stop hook captures transcript           | inject block
                  v                                         ^
   +--------------+---------------+         +---------------+--------------+
   | [1] EXTRACTION PIPELINE      |         | [6] COMPRESSION + INJECTION  |
   |   sanitize -> classify ->    |         |   authority-weighted budget, |
   |   salience (ONNX) ->         |         |   drop-to-fit (keep every    |
   |   decision-key + contracts   |         |   constraint) -> block       |
   +--------------+---------------+         +---------------+--------------+
                  | enqueue                                 | rank + fit
                  v                                         ^
   +--------------+---------------+         +---------------+--------------+
   | [2] WORKER + CONSOLIDATION   |         | [5] RETRIEVAL                |
   |   claim -> delta-merge ->    |         |   FTS5 BM25 + dense -> RRF   |
   |   supersede (latest-wins) -> |         |   prohibition_search (K1)    |
   |   project   (idempotent)     |         |   skeleton graph (PageRank)  |
   +--------------+---------------+         +---------------+--------------+
                  | persist (atomic)                        | recall
                  v                                         ^
   +--------------+-----------------------------------------+--------------+
   | [3] EVENT-SOURCED STORE   *   SQLite (WAL)                            |
   | typed events | evidence | provenance | FTS5 | embeddings | ledger     |
   +----------------------------------------------------------------------+
   | [4] RELIABILITY SPINE   atomic migrations | idempotent replay |       |
   | doctor --strict | fail-open hooks | import-linter | CI gate           |
   +----------------------------------------------------------------------+

四个钩面

CogniKernel 在四个点附加到会话。 每个都是失败打开的 - 如果内存不可用或出现错误，则挂钩会记录为“警告”，干净地返回，并且会话继续。

表面 Hook 权威 它的作用
会话块 会话开始 咨询 注入规范决策/约束/骨架块
CK-1召回 用户提示提交 咨询 表面提示相关内存、双证据门控、通过渲染账本进行重复数据删除
读/编辑门 预工具使用 硬/JIT Read/Grep 上的读取效率门；写入/编辑 (K2) 上出现即时禁止
捕获 Stop 副作用 提取并保存决策 - 你永远不会手动将内存写入 CLAUDE.md

什么被记住

记忆是键入的，而不是自由文本块。 类型驱动排名、渲染和取代：

  • DECISION — 做出的选择（“使用 Redis 作为速率限制器”）
  • CONSTRAINT_HARD / CONSTRAINT_SOFT — 规则，按道义力分级
  • APPROACH_ABANDONED_DO_NOT_RETRY — 死胡同，保留在墓地中，以便代理不会重新尝试
  • 约定、配置事实、模式决策（规范角色键）

决策键让稍后的重述取代先前的重述（最新获胜），因此商店会自我巩固而不是积累矛盾。 安装编码器后端时，可选的交叉编码器会添加语义取代。

检索

词汇主要，具有稠密的融合信号 — 绝不是纯向量：

  • 混合核心 — FTS5 BM25 ∪ 可选密集嵌入 → 倒数秩融合
  • hibition_search — 类型限制的词汇池，因此在您即将执行 X 操作时，“不要执行 X”规则不会被主题相似的散文所挤出
  • Skeleton — 按 PageRank 排名的 AST 符号图； find_ related unions 语义（嵌入）邻居与导入图相邻事件来显示更改所涉及的内容（语义轴需要额外的嵌入）
  • 阅读时的黄金记录整合——最新胜利的协调，因此回忆会返回一个连贯的答案，而不是一堆修订

它能为您节省什么

在三臂比较中进行基准测试 - CogniKernel 与平面策划笔记 vs 无记忆 - 在四个多会话项目中进行真实代理会话：

  • 文件内容如下：普遍胜利。 CogniKernel 手臂在每个项目中进行的文件读取次数最少，通常少 2-4 倍（23 vs 63、16 vs 47/53、40 vs 89/83），在最好的情况下，读取次数为 3 vs 29，因为注入的块 + AST 骨架承载了整个存储库的形状。 更少的读取意味着更少的工具往返和更多的上下文窗口留给实际工作——你的会话在压缩之前会变得更长，而不仅仅是更便宜。
  • 令牌：在内存很重要的情况下最多便宜 20%。 对于具有不断变化的决策和跨文件依赖关系的项目，价格加权代币成本（缓存写入 1.25×、缓存读取 0.1×、输出 5×）降低了 18-23%，而对于代码本身重读成本低廉的小型实施密集型项目来说，价格加权代币成本降低了 18-23%。 我们发布诚实的数字，而不是原始令牌的数字（原始数字看起来好约 30-40%，但任何会话的账单的约 95% 都是折扣缓存读取的）。
  • 回忆而不是重新推导。 记忆发挥作用的地方是那些状态太大、进化太多或寿命太长而无法廉价重新推导的项目：智能体开始已经知道决策、约束和死胡同，而不是花费会话的第一季度重新发现它们。

可靠性——脊柱

该系统被设计为清晰地降级，而不是静默降级：

  • 原子迁移——每个编号的迁移在一个事务中应用其主体+版本碰撞；脚本中途安全崩溃
  • 幂等重放——重新运行的工作人员工作不能重复计算或漂移衰减（证据来源守卫）
  • 故障打开钩子——每个表面都会吞下自己的故障并记录在警告处；沉默永远不会被解读为成功
  • memlora doctor --strict — 每个子系统的健康状况（模式、FTS5、嵌入、符号、工作队列）；降级时非零退出
  • 架构实施 - import-linter 分层合约，由元测试保护，因此拼写错误无法默默地禁用它们
  • CI 推广门 — lint + 全套（包括 每个 PR 的测试/可靠性/故障注入）；参见 CONTRIBUTING.md

跨平台（法典）

该存储是平台中立的 - 每个逻辑项目一个 SQLite DB，因此在同一目录中工作的 Claude Code 和 Codex 共享一个内存。 项目解析是别名感知的：C:\repo 和 /mnt/c/repo 解析为同一存储，因此内存遵循跨 Windows、WSL 和本机安装的签出；对于真正不同的结帐路径，.memlora/config.toml 中的一个选择加入的 project_identity 键将它们固定到一个共享存储。 Codex通过注册的MCP服务器读取内存（get_session_state/recall）；捕获方向是基于拉动的，因为 Codex 没有等效的 Stop-hook：

  • memlora codex-sync <project> 扫描 ~/.codex/sessions 中记录的 cwd 映射到项目的 rollout，并通过相同的提取管道捕获增量（rollout→transcript 适配器是唯一特定于 Codex 的代码；delta/dedup/idempotency 是共享的且未更改）。
  • 交接时自动 — Claude 的 SessionStart 在构建区块之前耗尽待处理的 Codex 推出，并且 MCP 服务器的队列耗尽器每个周期都会拉取新的推出，因此实时 Claude 会话无需等待下一个会话即可获取 Codex 端决策；在 Codex 方面，init 编写一条 AGENTS.md 指令 + 一个 ck-sync 技能，以便 Codex 在会话开始时拉取。
  • init 提供 — .mcp.json (Claude) 和 .codex/config.toml（固定服务器的 cwd + 项目环境）+ AGENTS.md (Codex)，幂等且不会破坏现有设置。
  • memlora doctor 报告了 Codex 运行状况线（会话目录 + 部署计数，或“没有要同步的内容”——Codex 是可选的，因此没有它是健康的）。

Codex 中做出的决定会到达下一个 Claude 会话的区块，反之亦然。 动作点表面（CK-1、PreToolUse 门）是 Claude-only 的 - Codex 没有每个提示/每个工具挂钩 - 因此在 Codex 上，循环降级为共享块 + MCP 调用。

接口

MCP 工具（会话块自动注入；这些用于有针对性的使用）：recall · find_lated · sculpture · get_session_state

CLI:

  • memlora init <project> — 注册项目并安装会话挂钩
  • memlora doctor [--strict] <project> — 子系统健康报告
  • memlora codex-sync <project> — 捕获该项目的 Codex CLI 会话
  • memlora install-heads — 安装经过训练的编码器工件（salience + 交叉编码器 ONNX 主体）：从 Heads-v1 版本下载并经过 sha256 验证，或从本地模型/导出（如果存在）复制
  • memlora show <project> / memlora reset <project> — 检查/清除存储的内存

快速入门

uv sync                      # core (lexical-only)
uv sync --extra embedding    # + dense retrieval (fastembed + numpy)

uv run memlora init .            # register this project + install hooks
uv run memlora doctor .          # subsystem health

uv run memlora install-heads     # optional: trained encoder heads (~270 MB download);
                                 # without them extraction/supersession fall back to
                                 # the legacy/lexical path — everything still works

然后在项目中启动 Claude Code 会话 - 内存块在会话开始时自动出现，并在会话结束时捕获决策。

项目布局

src/memlora/
  integration/   hooks, CLI, MCP server, session/worker orchestration
  extraction/    sanitize -> classify -> salience -> decision-key pipeline
  delta/         delta-merge + supersession (latest-wins; cross-encoder optional)
  retrieval/     hybrid BM25 + dense -> RRF
  storage/       event-sourced SQLite, FTS5, migrations, render ledger
  embedding/     optional dense vectors (fastembed)
  symbols/       AST skeleton + PageRank graph
  compression/   authority-weighted drop-to-fit budget
  injection/     block template assembly
  model.py       Event — the dependency-free domain primitive
tests/
  unit/          per-subsystem
  reliability/   crash-replay · worker-contention · corrupt-input injection

地位

Schema v18（包括 Codex 跨平台捕获）。 架构合同：3 个保留/0 个破坏。 CI 门：Ubuntu (3.11/3.12) 和 Windows 上的 lint + 完整套件。 请参阅 CONTRIBUTING.md 了解控制每个更改的“完成”定义。
